title: Pawia indica
taxonomy: Pawia indica is classified as a species of sea cucumber within the family Elpidiidae. It belongs to the order Elpidiida and the class Holothuroidea. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. Pawia indica is further categorized as a genus and species within the phylum Echinodermata, which also includes starfish and sea urchins. Its classification is an essential part of understanding its biology and role in marine ecosystems.

intro:
There is no information on a plant called "Pawia indica". However, I found "Pawia" might be a misspelling or variation of "Pawetta" or "Pawetta indica". "Pawetta indica" is also known as Indian calumba. 

Indian calumba is a plant used in traditional medicine. It is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems.

The bioactive constituents of Indian calumba include alkaloids, such as pawettine, pawetinine, and pawetine, as well as other compounds like sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids. These bioactive compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.

Indian calumba is used in various herbal preparations, including tinctures, decoctions, and infusions. In traditional medicine, it is often combined with other herbs to create remedies for digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
